topic.what: International standard for histological classification and grading of gastritis using a five-biopsy protocol and visual analogue scales.
topic.how: Standardized five-variable grading (inflammation, activity, atrophy, intestinal metaplasia, H. pylori density) on a four-tier scale with combined topographic-etiological-morphological framework.
topic.why_matters: The lingua franca of gastritis reporting worldwide — all subsequent classification systems (OLGA, OLGIM, Kyoto) build on it.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree of international adoption as standard
characteristics.value: Universal standard — adopted globally by pathologists
characteristics.vs_baseline: Replaced Schindler, Strickland-Mackay, and all prior systems

characteristics.what_it_measures: Agreement between different pathologists using the system
characteristics.value: Good for inflammation/H. pylori, moderate for atrophy

novelty: First internationally adopted standardized gastritis grading system
Combined topographic, morphological, and etiological classification
Visual analogue scales improved inter-observer agreement
open_problems: Atrophy grading has moderate inter-observer variability
Does not directly quantify cancer risk (OLGA/OLGIM addresses this)
Requires trained pathologist — not automatable as designed
